user_explanation: This is typically part of the manufacturer maintenance schedule and is usually appropriate when due by mileage or time.
description: Standard manufacturer-recommended maintenance item.

user_explanation: Oil filter replacement is commonly performed together with an engine oil change.
description: Usually performed together with an engine oil change.

user_explanation: Tire rotation is commonly recommended at regular intervals to promote even tire wear.
description: Standard maintenance item to promote even tire wear.

recommendation_text: May be needed depending on symptoms or tire wear.
user_explanation: Wheel alignment is usually recommended if the vehicle pulls, the steering wheel is off-center, or the tires are wearing unevenly.
description: Usually needed only if the vehicle pulls, steering is off-center, or tires show uneven wear.

recommendation_text: May be needed if vibration is present.
user_explanation: Tire balancing is usually relevant when there is vibration at speed or during tire installation or replacement.
description: Usually needed when there is vibration or during tire installation/replacement.

user_explanation: Cabin air filter replacement is a normal maintenance service that helps cabin airflow and HVAC performance.
description: Manufacturer maintenance item affecting cabin airflow and HVAC performance.

user_explanation: Engine air filter replacement is a standard maintenance item that helps maintain proper intake airflow.
description: Manufacturer maintenance item affecting engine intake airflow.

user_explanation: Spark plugs are a manufacturer maintenance item, but the mileage interval may vary depending on the engine.
description: Manufacturer maintenance item with longer replacement intervals.

user_explanation: Brake inspection is a normal and reasonable part of routine maintenance or service visits.
description: Legitimate inspection item commonly part of standard maintenance.

recommendation_text: May be needed depending on brake wear.
user_explanation: Brake pad replacement should be based on actual pad thickness and wear, not just time or mileage alone.
description: Needed based on actual brake pad wear, not just time.

recommendation_text: May be needed depending on rotor condition.
user_explanation: Brake rotor replacement is usually justified only when rotors are worn below specification, damaged, or heavily scored.
description: Needed based on rotor thickness, wear, scoring, or damage.

recommendation_text: May be needed depending on age or fluid condition.
user_explanation: Brake fluid service can be legitimate depending on vehicle age, moisture content, maintenance history, or test results.
description: Can be a legitimate service depending on age, moisture content, or maintenance interval.

recommendation_text: May be useful in harsh climates or during brake service.
user_explanation: Brake cleaning and lubrication is not always a standard scheduled item, but it can be reasonable in winter or road-salt conditions such as Ontario.
description: May be reasonable in harsh climates or during brake service, but not always a standard scheduled item.

user_explanation: Battery terminal cleaning is a legitimate minor service when corrosion is visible on the battery terminals.
description: Valid service if terminal corrosion is present.

recommendation_text: May be needed if battery health test is poor.
user_explanation: Battery replacement is usually appropriate when a battery test shows weak performance or failure.
description: Legitimate when battery health test shows failure or weak performance.

user_explanation: Engine coolant replacement is a long-interval maintenance item and should be checked against vehicle age and mileage.
description: Manufacturer maintenance item with long intervals.

recommendation_text: May be needed depending on vehicle policy, mileage, and transmission type.
user_explanation: Transmission fluid service can be legitimate, but it should be evaluated using vehicle-specific maintenance guidance and driving conditions.
description: Legitimate in some cases depending on maintenance policy, mileage, usage, and transmission type.

recommendation_text: May be needed depending on drivetrain and usage.
user_explanation: Differential fluid service is only relevant for vehicles with the applicable drivetrain components and may depend on use conditions.
description: May be needed based on drivetrain and service conditions.

recommendation_text: May be needed depending on drivetrain and usage.
user_explanation: Transfer case fluid service is only relevant for vehicles equipped with a transfer case and may depend on service conditions.
description: May be needed depending on drivetrain and use case.

recommendation_text: Usually symptom-based rather than routine maintenance.
user_explanation: AC recharge is typically appropriate only when cooling performance is poor and the system has been diagnosed properly.
description: Usually symptom-based and should not be sold routinely without diagnosis.

recommendation_text: Usually not part of standard maintenance.
user_explanation: Consider this only if there are drivability symptoms such as rough idle, misfire, or poor fuel economy.
description: Typically not part of standard manufacturer maintenance and often sold as an optional service.

recommendation_text: Usually not part of standard maintenance.
user_explanation: Throttle body cleaning is generally optional unless there are symptoms such as rough idle or airflow-related drivability issues.
description: Typically not part of standard scheduled maintenance.

recommendation_text: Usually an optional service, not routine maintenance.
user_explanation: Engine decarbon service is usually marketed as an add-on and should only be considered if there is a clear symptom or manufacturer guidance.
description: Usually an optional upsell rather than a standard maintenance item.

user_explanation: Induction service is not typically part of routine maintenance and should be questioned unless there is a specific reason.
description: Usually marketed as an optional cleaning service, not routine maintenance.

recommendation_text: Usually not part of standard maintenance.
user_explanation: Fuel system flush is generally an optional add-on and not a standard scheduled service.
description: Usually optional and not a standard scheduled service.

user_explanation: Premium oil additives are often upsell items and are usually unnecessary when the correct engine oil is already used.
description: Usually an upsell item, not standard maintenance.

user_explanation: Fuel additives are commonly sold as optional extras and are generally not part of standard maintenance.
description: Usually optional and not part of standard scheduled maintenance.

user_explanation: A multi-point inspection is generally useful as supporting information, but by itself it is not a repair recommendation.
description: A general inspection item, often free or bundled.

user_explanation: Campaign check means the shop looked for open service campaigns or service actions.
description: A check for open campaigns or service actions.

user_explanation: Recall check means the shop looked for open recalls on the vehicle.
description: A check for open recalls.

user_explanation: TPMS reset is usually a small supporting action performed during tire service.
description: A minor tire-system setup/reset item, often bundled.

user_explanation: Washer fluid top-up is a small consumable add-on or bundled service item.
description: Minor consumable top-up item.

recommendation_text: Legitimate if there is a warning light or symptom.
user_explanation: Diagnostic service is usually appropriate when the vehicle has a fault, warning light, or clear drivability issue.
description: Legitimate when a fault, warning light, or symptom exists.
